notes.anchor: the eleventh day before the Kalends of June
notes.note: The Romans counted dates inclusively backward from fixed points; the Kalends was the first of the month, so this is 22 May 51 BC.
A legatus was a senior deputy appointed to assist a governor or general with military and administrative authority; Vergilianus served Appius in this role in Cilicia.

notes.anchor: a stronger garrison force was needed for that province
notes.note: Cilicia lay exposed to a feared Parthian invasion after the disaster of Crassus at Carrhae (53 BC); the Senate's anxiety about reinforcements reflects this threat on the eastern frontier.
Servius Sulpicius Rufus, consul in 51 BC and the foremost jurist of the age, who blocked the proposed levy of reinforcements for the eastern provinces.

notes.anchor: the decrees of the Senate that have been passed
notes.note: Senatus consulta: formal resolutions of the Senate, here governing the disposition of troops in the eastern provinces.
